key_findings: workmux backend detection uses env-var cascade: $WORKMUX_BACKEND (override) → $TMUX (tmux) → $WEZTERM_PANE (wezterm) → $ZELLIJ (zellij) → $KITTY_WINDOW_ID (kitty) → default tmux. Pure function resolve_backend() separated for testability. Directly adoptable for openswarm.
workmux Zellij support requires building Zellij from source (unreleased features: --pane-id targeting, close-tab-by-id, go-to-tab-by-id). Not production-ready as of March 2026.

workmux is Rust with a Multiplexer trait abstraction (src/multiplexer/mod.rs). ~40 method interface: create_window, split_pane, send_keys, capture_pane, set_status, create_handshake, etc. Named-pipe handshake pattern for shell startup synchronization.
workmux is git-worktree-centric (one worktree per agent/task), providing file isolation between parallel agents. NTM has no such isolation — agents share the working tree.
NTM is tmux-only (intentionally), Go binary, REST/SSE/WebSocket API via 'ntm serve', machine-readable '--robot-*' flag surface. Named pane pattern: <project>__<agent>_<number>.
NTM has a safety system (policy rules, approval workflows, destructive command blocking) and durable state (checkpoints, timelines, audit logs, pipeline YAML with resume).
NTM context rotation: monitors token usage estimates, auto-rotates agents at 95% threshold with handoff summary. First-class primitive for long-running swarms.
NTM work assignment strategies: balanced, speed, quality, dependency — integrates with 'br'/'bv' work graph tools for intelligent task routing.
Both tools converge on tmux as the only battle-tested backend. WezTerm/Kitty/Zellij are experimental in workmux; absent in NTM.

For openswarm: adopt workmux's env-var backend detection, workmux's Multiplexer trait pattern, NTM's robot-mode JSON surface design, NTM's REST/WebSocket API model, and workmux's git-worktree isolation concept.
